This document has no headings. It is a plain sequence of paragraphs with no
structural hierarchy whatsoever.

Embedding models convert text into high-dimensional vectors. These vectors
capture semantic meaning, allowing similar texts to be found even when they use
entirely different words. For example, "automobile" and "car" would have vectors
that are close together in the embedding space.

The quality of an embedding model depends on the training data and the objective
function used during training. Models trained with contrastive objectives on
paired examples tend to produce better retrieval-oriented embeddings than models
trained purely on language modeling objectives.
